mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales reflects strengthening consumer demand that translates into higher corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following month-over-month RSAFS increases are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.5 percent indicating tight labor market conditions, wage pressures reduce corporate profit margins and trigger negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Instances where UNRATE is below 5.5% are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of occurrences over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising consumer prices and falling industrial production creates a stagflationary environment where input costs increase without output growth, depressing S&P 500 equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases month-over-month and INDPRO decreases month-over-month
failure_condition: The interaction between CPIAUCSL and INDPRO does not significantly predict negative 20-day returns (p > 0.05) or produces a Rank IC below 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a low interest-rate environment, an increase in the federal funds rate signals policy normalization that reduces equity risk premiums, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods when FEDFUNDS is below 1%, month-over-month increases are followed by negative 20-day returns in less than 55% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Acceleration in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing capacity utilization that supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in INDPRO do not produce positive 5-day S&P 500 returns with Rank IC above 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts below 600 thousand units signals weakening residential investment and broader demand contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 600, 60-day forward S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 50% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in nonfarm payrolls and increases in unemployment confirm labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending forecasts and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS decreases month-over-month and UNRATE increases month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint decline in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE does not produce significantly negative 20-day S&P 500 returns (t-test p > 0.05) with Rank IC below 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects aggregate demand expansion that boosts broad corporate profitability and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 increases are not followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.
